author: @MichaelSkinner-e9j
text:
Here’s something I want to know-

How does graphene fail?
Is it like carbon fiber and it’s just catastrophic?  Or is there any kind of flex to it?

replies.author: @systematicmeansllc
replies.text: Like carbon fiber it has an immense tensile strength, exhibiting essentially crystalline metal atom-to-atom bonds. However its compression modulus is horrible along it’s plane, same as CF. Extremely brittle, buckling and shattering very easily.

replies.text:
Wouldn’t it just be co2? When heated it will just turn to a gas. Right? 
Since a co2 gas is clear I think, the laser probably wouldn’t heat it up. 
Also you would need to split off the oxygen, which from the brief reading I did looks to be troublesome.
